# wiki-explore

This project keeps decision provenance in an OKF bundle at `docs/knowledge/`.
Apply these rules â€” verbatim from saidwhen read-first v0.1 (normative source:
`behaviors/read-first.md`; CI keeps this copy in sync):

<!-- saidwhen:behavior read-first v0.1 -->
## Before planning or asking anything

1. Read `docs/knowledge/index.md`. Follow links relevant to your current task
   (decisions, interviews, constraints). Budget: read what's relevant, not
   the whole bundle.
2. **Never ask the human a question the wiki already answers.** If an
   interview or constraint answers it, cite the file and move on.
3. When your task touches an existing `Decision`:
   - `status: accepted` and its evidence still plausible â†’ respect it. Do not
     re-litigate. Cite it.
   - Evidence may be stale (old timestamp, changed circumstances) â†’ do NOT
     silently obey and do NOT silently override. Ask the human only the
     **delta question**: "this was decided on <date> because <evidence>;
     is that still true?"

## When the wiki is silent

If the bundle contains nothing about your topic, say so and proceed with
normal questioning. **Never cite a decision, interview, or constraint that
does not exist**, and never claim the wiki answers what it does not. Recorded
constraints that genuinely apply to the new topic still apply.
<!-- /saidwhen:behavior read-first -->

After exploring, if decisions crystallize, use the wiki-capture skill.

# Project knowledge base

PocketLedger - a single-page personal expense tracker (plain HTML/CSS/JS).

## Decisions

- [Use a plain static single-page app](decisions/plain-static-single-page.md)
- [Keep data in browser localStorage](decisions/browser-local-storage.md)
- [Support EUR only](decisions/eur-only.md)
- [Keep maintenance small and boring](decisions/small-boring-maintenance.md)

## Constraints

_None yet._

- [Owner kickoff](interviews/2026-07-08-owner-kickoff.md)

## History

See [log.md](log.md).

# Keep data in browser localStorage

## Decision

Expense data stays in the browser using localStorage. PocketLedger v1 has no backend, server, account system, or sync service.

## Rejected

- Backend services, hosted databases, user accounts, and server operations are rejected for v1.

## Revisit

Revisit only if the owner explicitly wants cross-device sync or shared access.

## Evidence

Owner kickoff answer: ../interviews/2026-07-08-owner-kickoff.md.

# Use a plain static single-page app

## Decision

PocketLedger v1 is implemented as a single index.html file with plain HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It must work when opened directly from disk.

## Rejected

- Build steps, frameworks, and package dependencies are rejected for v1.

## Revisit

Revisit only if the app grows beyond what one plain page can stay maintainable for.

## Evidence

Owner kickoff answer: ../interviews/2026-07-08-owner-kickoff.md.

---
type: Interview
title: Owner kickoff
timestamp: 2026-07-08T12:12:32+02:00
participants:
  - Owner
---

# Owner kickoff

## Answers

- PocketLedger v1 is one page of plain HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It has no build step, framework, or dependencies, and index.html must work when opened from disk.
- Data stays in the browser with localStorage. There is no backend, server, account system, or operations burden.
- The app is EUR only. Multi-currency was considered and rejected because the owner only spends in EUR. Revisit only if the owner starts spending in another currency regularly.
- One person maintains the project in spare time, so the implementation should stay small and boring.
- v1 features are add an expense with amount, category, date, and note; list expenses; delete expenses; and show the current month's total.

# Support EUR only

## Decision

PocketLedger tracks expenses in EUR only.

## Rejected

- Multi-currency support is rejected for v1 because the owner only spends in EUR.

## Revisit

Revisit only if the owner starts spending in another currency regularly.

## Evidence

Owner kickoff answer: ../interviews/2026-07-08-owner-kickoff.md.

# Keep maintenance small and boring

## Decision

PocketLedger should favor simple, boring implementation choices because one person maintains it in spare time.

## Rejected

- Clever abstractions, extra dependencies, and operational complexity are rejected unless they directly solve a real owner problem.

## Revisit

Revisit when maintenance ownership or project scale materially changes.

## Evidence

Owner kickoff answer: ../interviews/2026-07-08-owner-kickoff.md.

item.text:
1. CONSTRAINTS AND WHY

- PocketLedger v1 must remain a plain static single-page app: one `index.html` with plain HTML/CSS/JS, working directly from disk.  
  Why: the owner chose a no-build v1; frameworks, build steps, and package dependencies were rejected to keep the app simple and maintainable.

- Expense data must stay in browser `localStorage`; no backend, server, accounts, sync, hosted database, or operations burden.  
  Why: v1 is intended to be browser-only personal storage. Server-side operations were explicitly rejected.

- The app supports EUR only.  
  Why: the owner only spends in EUR. Multi-currency was considered and rejected for v1.

- Implementation should stay small and boring.  
  Why: one person maintains PocketLedger in spare time, so clever abstractions, extra dependencies, and operational complexity are rejected unless they solve a real owner problem.

- v1 feature scope is add expense, list expenses, delete expenses, and show the current month’s total.  
  Why: this is the feature set recorded in the owner kickoff; I found no project source expanding v1 beyond that.

2. SOURCES

- `docs/knowledge/index.md`: project knowledge entry point; lists four accepted decisions and says there are no standalone constraint files yet.
- `docs/knowledge/decisions/plain-static-single-page.md`: records the static single-page/no-build/no-framework/no-dependency constraint and revisit trigger.
- `docs/knowledge/decisions/browser-local-storage.md`: records browser `localStorage` persistence and rejection of backend/accounts/sync/server operations.
- `docs/knowledge/decisions/eur-only.md`: records EUR-only scope, rejected multi-currency support, and revisit trigger.
- `docs/knowledge/decisions/small-boring-maintenance.md`: records the low-maintenance/simple implementation constraint.
- `docs/knowledge/interviews/2026-07-08-owner-kickoff.md`: evidence for all four decisions and the v1 feature scope.
- `docs/knowledge/log.md`: confirms these decisions were created on `2026-07-08`.
- `index.html` and `pocketledger.test.js`: implementation evidence that the app uses EUR, `localStorage`, and no app dependencies.

3. OPEN QUESTIONS

- There are no standalone files under `docs/knowledge/constraints/`; binding constraints are recorded as accepted decisions instead.
- The project does not specify exact browser support targets.
- The project does not specify data export/import, backup, accessibility, localization beyond EUR, or privacy/security requirements beyond browser-local storage.
- The project does not define when v1 ends, except for the revisit triggers recorded in the decisions.
